$comment: MCP connectors for the loop. The GitHub server lets the loop open PRs (never merge).
WINDOWS GOTCHA — load-bearing: an npx-based MCP server MUST be launched through
the `cmd /c` wrapper on Windows. `npx` is a .cmd shim, and Windows will not spawn
it directly from an MCP client the way it spawns a real .exe — wire it as a bare
`npx ...` and the server fails SILENTLY (no error, the tools just never appear).
Wrapping as `cmd /c npx ...` is what makes it actually start.
The non-Windows form is just `command: npx, args: [-y, ...]`. Shown below is the
Windows-correct form because this repo is developed on Windows.
